title: binghamia multangularis var. rufispina
intro: Binghamia multangularis var. rufispina, also known as Binghamia rufispina, is a plant species in the family Mimosaceae, native to Australia. It is a shrub-like tree that is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which have been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine. The plant has been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The seeds of Binghamia rufispina can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which are commonly used to promote healing and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
